title: Syringa pubescens subsp. patula
what_description: Syringa pubescens subsp. patula is an ornamental shrub belonging to the family Oleaceae. It is characterized by its small, fragrant flowers with a white color, produced in terminal panicles, and its leaves are lanceolate to ovate, 2-5 cm long, with a hairy surface. The plant grows up to 2-3 meters tall, with a spread of 1.5-2.5 meters, and has a deciduous habit. Syringa pubescens subsp. patula is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and is often cultivated for its ornamental value. Its growth rate is moderate to fast, and it prefers well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade conditions.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Syringa pubescens subsp. patula are inflammation, anxiety and hypertension, which are alleviated due to the plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The same flavonoids also show anxiolytic effects, reducing anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters. Furthermore, Syringa pubescens subsp. patula's saponins have been shown to lower blood pressure and reduce fever, making it effective in treating fever. Additionally, its essential oils exhibit antifungal properties, treating fungal infections and skin irritation. Its use also has been linked to improved sleep quality in individuals with insomnia and stress, as well as aiding in the management of diabetes, insect bites, inflammatory arthritis, digestive issues, depression, cold sores, diarrhea, wounds, asthma, pain, mouth ulcers and eczema.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Syringa pubescens subsp. patula are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils in the plant. These bioactive compounds possess antioxidant properties, contributing to the plant's ability to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory properties of Syringa pubescens subsp. patula are also noteworthy, as it has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation. Furthermore, the analgesic properties of the plant are attributed to the presence of saponins, which have been shown to possess pain-relieving activities. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of Syringa pubescens subsp. patula are significant, as it has been found to inhibit the growth of various bacterial strains, making it a potential natural remedy for bacterial infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Syringa pubescens subsp. patula are syringin, flavonoids, quercetin, phenolic acids, and kaempferol. Syringin has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its wound-healing activity. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect cells from damage and reduce inflammation. Phenolic acids, a subgroup of flavonoids, possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, further enhancing the plant's healing potential. The combined action of these constituents makes Syringa pubescens subsp. patula a valuable source of natural compounds for wound healing and other medical applications.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Syringa pubescens subsp. patula are tincture, infusion, tea, salve, and ointment. The tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems, typically used for internal and external treatment of various health issues. The infusion is a liquid extract of the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems in hot water, commonly used to treat respiratory issues such as colds and bronchitis. The salve and ointment are topical preparations used to treat skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and dermatitis, by applying the plant's extracts to the affected area. These preparations are typically made using the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems, which are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Syringa pubescens subsp. patula are numerous and can be severe if not used properly. Syringa pubescens subsp. patula, also known as the "Mexican lilac", contains compounds that can induce allergic reaction, cause diarrhea, and develop skin irritation in some individuals, possibly due to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. Furthermore, the plant's essential oils may also induce nausea and cause stomach discomfort, especially if ingested in large quantities. The severity of these side effects can vary depending on the method of preparation, dosage, and individual tolerance. Improper use of Syringa pubescens subsp. patula, such as ingestion without proper preparation or in excess, can exacerbate these side effects and lead to more severe health issues.
